thesis: AI-era cloud observability platform. Q1 2026 revenue crossed $1B for first time (32% YoY growth). Beat EPS by 17.65%. Secular tailwind from AI workload monitoring across multi-cloud enterprises. Analyst PTs range $180-$305. Goldman Sell (PT $139) is an outlier; most analysts bullish. Long-term: 6-12 months as AI infrastructure spend accelerates.

Madrigal Pharmaceuticals — maker of Rezdiffra, the only FDA-approved therapy for MASH (metabolic-associated steatohepatitis, formerly NASH). First-mover advantage in a large unmet-need indication. Truist raised PT to $709 (May 13), Evercore Outperform PT $649. 40% upside to Truist PT. Risk: FDA safety signal or commercial uptake slower than expected.

Occidental Petroleum — Berkshire Hathaway-backed energy major. Hormuz Strait closure (since Feb 28) creating structural floor for Brent crude ($104-111). UAE exit from OPEC adds further supply uncertainty. OXY leveraged to higher oil prices with Permian Basin assets and chemical segment. 52-week high $67.45 implies 20%+ upside from entry. Risk: Hormuz reopens or global demand collapses.

Visa — global payments network with structural moat. US-China summit (Trump in Beijing with CEOs) positive for cross-border trade volumes. Truist raised PT to $371 (May 12), implying 15% upside. Kevin Warsh replacing Powell signals potentially more accommodative Fed stance. Global GDP growth drives payment volumes. Risk: recession causes consumer spending pullback.

Walmart — dominant global consumer staples retailer at $1T market cap. Advertising business grew 46% in FY2026, transforming WMT into a retail media platform (like Amazon). Consumer confidence at multi-year low benefits value retailers as shoppers trade down. Morgan Stanley maintains Overweight ahead of Q1 2026 earnings. US-China summit positive for supply chain. Stop-loss order failed to place pre-market — must be added manually post-fill. Risk: tariff pass-through limits margin, Amazon competition intensifies.
